enableTraitTokens.name: Enable Trait Tokens
enableTraitTokens.hint: Drag trait items onto the canvas to create post-it note tokens. Requires a world reload when changed.

showInfoButtons.name: Show Info Buttons
showInfoButtons.hint: Show info buttons next to Stress Track, Determination, and Values headers that display quick reference popups.

enableFatigue.name: Enable Fatigue Management
enableFatigue.hint: Enable automatic fatigue trait creation when stress is maxed, fatigue notices in chat and dice pool dialogs, and fatigued attribute display on character sheets. Requires a world reload when changed.

playerAmbientAudioSelectionOnly.name: Player Ambient Audio Uses Selected Tokens
playerAmbientAudioSelectionOnly.hint: If enabled, non-GM users only hear ambient audio near tokens they currently have selected/controlled (matching GM behavior). If disabled, Foundry's default behavior applies (players hear audio near all owned tokens).

enableBacklinks.name: Enable Journal Backlinks
enableBacklinks.hint: Add wiki-style 'referenced by' links to journals, actors, and items that reference each other. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableStyleEnhance.name: Enable Style Enhancements
enableStyleEnhance.hint: Apply additional CSS styling tweaks to STA sheets and chat cards. Takes effect immediately.

enableTalentAutomations.name: Enable Talent Automations
enableTalentAutomations.hint: Automatically apply effects based on talents a character possesses (e.g. modify attributes, post reminders). Requires a world reload when changed.

disableTooltips.name: Disable Item Tooltips
disableTooltips.hint: Hide the item description tooltip popups that appear when hovering over item names on actor sheets.

enableActionChooser.name: Enable Conflict Actions Window
enableActionChooser.hint: Show a window listing available actions, roll info, and Momentum costs during conflict scenes. Requires a world reload when changed.

enableMomentumSpend.name: Enable Momentum Spend
enableMomentumSpend.hint: Add a 'Spend Momentum' button to task roll chat messages that opens a momentum spend dialog. Requires a world reload when changed.

autoDeductMomentum.name: Auto-deduct Momentum
autoDeductMomentum.hint: Automatically deduct momentum from the shared pool when spending via the Momentum Spend dialog.

traits.fatiguedTooltip: Mark this Trait as a Fatigue trait (auto-created when stress is maxed).
traits.chooseAttributeTooltip: Choose which attribute your character is fatigued in.

stress.breatherDesc: Rest for a few minutes. Recover 4 stress.
stress.breakDesc: Rest for 30 minutes to a couple hours. Recover 8 Stress.
stress.sleepDesc: A full night's rest or similar amount of time. Recover all stress.
stress.momentumDesc: Recover 1 stress per 2 Momentum spent after a successful task, max 3.

determination.gainSpend: Gain or Spend Determination by using values.
determination.spendCrit: Spend determination before rolling for a guaranteed crit.
determination.spendReroll: Spend determination after rolling to reroll any failed dice.

valuesInfo.intro: Use Values to gain or spend determination:
valuesInfo.positiveDesc: Spend determination when your value is motivating you.
valuesInfo.negativeDesc: Gain determination when your value causes a complication.
valuesInfo.challengedDesc: Gain determination when you act against your values and change them.

talentsInfo.desc: Talents give you special abilities that let you do things outside the normal rules.

focusesInfo.desc: If a focus applies, dice will crit when they are equal to or less than your discipline used in the task.

traitsInfo.intro: If a trait is both TRUE and IMPORTANT, it can:

injuriesInfo.desc: Injuries are negative traits. Removing them requires a task roll with a difficulty equal to the severity of the attack that caused them.

logsInfo.desc2: Logs track which values you used in a mission.
logsInfo.desc3: String together multiple missions using the same value by making a callback during play to earn milestone advancements.

milestonesInfo.desc1: Milestones are improvements to your character.
milestonesInfo.desc2: Earn them by making a callback to another mission using the same value as the current task roll.

directiveInfo.desc: Directives are group values, and can be used in all the same ways as a value. Ask the GM what your Mission Directives are.

dicePoolMonitor.title: Dice Pool Monitor
dicePoolMonitor.gmOnly: Only the GM can use the dice pool monitor.
dicePoolMonitor.waitingForPlayers: Waiting for players to open dice pool dialogs…

warpCalculator.title: Warp Speed Calculator
warpCalculator.instructions: Enter any two values and leave the third blank to calculate it.
warpCalculator.enterTwoValues: Enter any two values above to calculate the third.
warpCalculator.cannotCalculate: Cannot calculate with the given values.

stardateCalculator.title: Stardate Calculator
stardateCalculator.instructions: Convert between TNG stardates and calendar dates.
stardateCalculator.enterDate: Enter a calendar date above to calculate the stardate.
stardateCalculator.enterStardate: Enter a stardate above to calculate the calendar date.

sceneTraitsActor.name: Scene Traits Actor
sceneTraitsActor.hint: Choose an existing Scene Traits actor for this scene, or leave as auto-create to generate one when first needed.

rebuildOnSave.name: Rebuild links on save
rebuildOnSave.hint: Automatically rebuild reference links whenever a journal page, actor, or item is saved.

headingTag.name: Backlinks heading tag
headingTag.hint: HTML heading element for the backlinks section (e.g. h1, h2, h3).

minPermission.name: Backlinks minimum permission
minPermission.hint: A user needs at least this permission level on the target document for a backlink to be visible.

crewManifest.folderHint: The manifest will include all actors in this folder and its subfolders, grouped and color-coded by subfolder.
crewManifest.customizeHint: Check or uncheck categories and individual crew members to include in the manifest.
crewManifest.subtitle: Crew Manifest
crewManifest.noFolders: No Actor folders found. Create a folder of actors first.
crewManifest.noActors: No actors found in the selected folder.
crewManifest.folderNotFound: The selected folder could not be found.

createOpportunity.name: Create Opportunity
createOpportunity.description: Before you attempt a task (but after the GM sets its Difficulty), build your dice pool, starting with 2d20. Add bonus d20s granted from talents and other game effects. Add more d20s by spending Momentum or adding Threat. The first bonus die costs 1, the second costs 2 more, and the third costs 3 more. You can't roll more than 5d20 on any task attempt.

alterTrait.name: Create/Change Trait
alterTrait.description: You can establish a new trait in the scene, or change, increase the Potency of, or remove a trait currently in play. A trait you create must relate to the task you just passed, and it must be something that would result from your actions.

reduceTime.name: Reduce Time
reduceTime.description: You can spend 2 Momentum to reduce the time it takes to complete the activity represented by the task, typically taking half the normal amount of time. You can't use this option during an action scene.

extraMajorAction.name: Extra Major Action
extraMajorAction.description: During your turn in an action scene, you may spend 2 Momentum to take an additional major action. However, any task roll attempted as part of this action increases in Difficulty by 1.

extraMinorAction.name: Extra Minor Action
extraMinorAction.description: During your turn in an action scene, you may spend 1 Momentum to take an additional minor action. Each minor action may only be performed once per turn.

addedSeverity.name: Added Severity
addedSeverity.description: Increase the severity of a successful attack by 1 for every 2 Momentum spent.

buyD20s.name: Buy D20s
buyD20s.description: Before attempting a task (but after the GM sets its Difficulty), start your dice pool with 2d20. Add bonus d20s granted from talents and other game effects. Add more d20s by spending Momentum or adding Threat. The first bonus die costs 1, the second costs 2 more, and the third costs 3 more. You can't roll more than 5d20 on a task attempt.

disarm.name: Disarm
disarm.description: Target drops one weapon they are holding to within their Reach. This costs 1 Momentum if the weapon is held in one hand. If the weapon is two-handed, the cost increases to 2 Momentum.

keepInitiative.name: Keep The Initiative
keepInitiative.description: At the end of your turn, pass the next turn to an ally instead of an enemy. Once that ally has acted, the next turn must go to an enemy.

obtainInformation.name: Obtain Information
obtainInformation.description: You can ask the GM a single question about the scene. The question must be related to the task attempted, and the GM must answer truthfully but doesn't have to give complete information. If there's no information your character could determine, the GM may answer 'you don't know,' but they must refund the Momentum spent for that question.

recoveringStress.name: Recovering Stress
recoveringStress.description: After a successful task, you may spend 2 Momentum to remove 1 Stress (Repeatable) from yourself or one ally who can hear you. A character cannot remove more than 3 Stress in this way on any single task.

addedDamage.name: Added Damage
addedDamage.description: Increase the damage of a successful attack by 1 for every 2 Momentum spent.

devastatingAttack.name: Devastating Attack
devastatingAttack.description: Roll an additional system; that system suffers a hit dealing half the attack's damage, rounding up.

actionChooser.title: Conflict Actions
actionChooser.noActor: Select a token or set an active character before using an action.
actionChooser.noStarship: Select a starship before using this action.

evidence.name: Evidence
evidence.description: Offer proof to support your claims. Each piece of evidence acts as a trait, allowing a Persuasion task or reducing its Difficulty by 1. Evidence can complement other social tools and each accepted piece adds 1 to the severity after an opposed Persuade task.

intimidation.name: Intimidation
intimidation.description: Use threats to compel action by inspiring fear and doubt. This is an opposed task whose Difficulty reflects each side's perceived strength. Success imposes a fear-based trait on the target; failure makes further intimidation harder. Intimidation is inherently hostile and may worsen other interactions or provoke aggression.

negotiation.name: Negotiation
negotiation.description: Offer compensation in exchange for a request. Each offer creates a trait representing what you give and a complication representing its cost, counting as a new circumstance for a Persuasion task. No task is required for the offer itself, but the cost of success may exceed expectations, and failing to deliver on promises can cause serious problems.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help 2 other characters per round instead of just 1. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. battle plans, strategies, inspiration, etc.).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take an action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

direct.name: Direct
direct.description: Spend 1 Momentum and select one ally on the bridge who immediately attempts a single Major Action. Assist them by rolling 1d20 with your Control + Command.

rally.name: Rally
rally.description: Roll Presence + Command vs Difficulty 0. Generate momentum to spend or save.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. coordinating personnel, piercing interference, encryption and decryption).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

damageControl.name: Damage Control
damageControl.description: Direct a team to patch a breach. Roll Presence + Engineering vs Difficulty 2, plus 1 for each degree of potency. Breach is temporarily removed on success.

transport.name: Transport
transport.description: Teleport people or objects. Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science. Base Difficulty 0. +1 if away from transporter room. +1 if Site A is not a transporter pad. +1 if Site B is not a transporter pad.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

impulse.name: Impulse
impulse.description: Move 2 zones. If you only move 1 zone, you may reduce the Momentum cost of movement through difficult or hazardous terrain by 1.

thrusters.name: Thrusters
thrusters.description: Move around within your current zone. You may make Contact with another ship safely, such as docking or landing.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. careful positioning, skilled maneuvering).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

attackPattern.name: Attack Pattern
attackPattern.description: Assist each attack until your next turn with Control + Conn. All incoming attacks have difficulty reduced by 1 until your next turn.

evasiveAction.name: Evasive Action
evasiveAction.description: Until your next turn, all incoming attacks are opposed rolls vs your Daring + Conn assisted by the ship's Structure + Conn. If you win the opposed roll, you may move the ship 1 zone. All attacks made by your ship suffer +1 Difficulty until your next turn. You may not take this action at the same time as Defensive Fire.

maneuver.name: Maneuver
maneuver.description: Roll Control + Conn vs Difficulty 0 to generate momentum, especially when crossing difficult terrain.

ram.name: Ram
ram.description: Move into Contact with a target at Close range. Perform an Attack with Daring + Conn assisted by the ship's Engines + Conn vs Difficulty 2. Each ship suffers damage equal to the other's scale + half the number of zones moved before collision. This attack is Devastating, Piercing, and Intense.

warp.name: Warp
warp.description: Requires Reserve Power. You must take a Prepare minor action first. Roll Control + Conn assisted by the ship's Engines + Conn vs Difficulty 1. On success, move a number of zones equal to Engines or leave the battlefield.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. plotting a careful course, studying the terrain).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. modifications to ship systems).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

damageControl.name: Damage Control
damageControl.description: Repair a damaged or disabled system. Roll Presence + Engineering assisted by the ship's Structure + Engineering vs Difficulty 2. A damaged system is repaired. A disabled system becomes damaged instead.

regainPower.name: Regain Power
regainPower.description: Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Engines + Engineering vs Difficulty 1. On success, regain 1 Reserve Power, plus 1 per Momentum spent (Repeatable).

regenerateShields.name: Regenerate Shields
regenerateShields.description: Requires Reserve Power. Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Structure + Engineering vs Difficulty 2. On success, regain shields equal to the ship's Security score.

reroutePower.name: Reroute Power
reroutePower.description: No roll required. Move Reserve Power from Engines to any other system, or vice versa. You may not move power from other systems.

transport.name: Transport
transport.description: Teleport people or objects. Roll Control + Engineering assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science. Base Difficulty 0. +1 if away from transporter room. +1 if Site A is not a transporter pad. +1 if Site B is not a transporter pad.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

calibrateSensors.name: Calibrate Sensors
calibrateSensors.description: Recalibrate the ship's sensors. The next Sensor Sweep or Scan for Weakness task reduces its Difficulty by 1.

launchProbe.name: Launch Probe
launchProbe.description: Launch a sensor probe to a zone within Long range. Until the probe is destroyed or retrieved, all tasks to detect or scan that zone reduce their Difficulty by 1.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. extra information gathered from sensor scans).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

reveal.name: Reveal
reveal.description: Scan a ship or zone to reveal all of its traits, including hidden ones. Roll Reason + Science assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science vs Difficulty 3.

scanForWeakness.name: Scan for Weakness
scanForWeakness.description: Opposed roll: Control + Science assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science vs the target's Daring + Security assisted by its Structure + Security. On success, the next attack against the target may re-roll any number of challenge dice on the damage roll.

sensorSweep.name: Sensor Sweep
sensorSweep.description: Scan for nearby objects, energy signatures, and anomalies. Roll Reason + Science assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science vs Difficulty 1. On success, learn basic information; spend Momentum for more detail.

changePosition.name: Change Position
changePosition.description: Move to another station. You must wait for the station to be unmanned before taking control. Immediate if the station is in the same room, otherwise, arrive at the start of the next turn.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Perform a task not otherwise listed. Complex actions require a major action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Get something ready for a rolled task and establish a narrative position to do it.

restore.name: Restore
restore.description: Resolve minor narrative issues with ship systems.

calibrateWeapons.name: Calibrate Weapons
calibrateWeapons.description: Recalibrate the ship's weapons. Your next attack with ship weapons may re-roll a single d20 on the task roll.

targetingSolution.name: Targeting Solution
targetingSolution.description: Lock onto a target. Your next attack against that target reduces its Difficulty by 1.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help another character. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 to create a trait, change a trait, or remove a trait (e.g. weapon modifications and targeting solutions).

override.name: Override
override.description: Take a major action from a non-Command station at +1 Difficulty.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

defensiveFire.name: Defensive Fire
defensiveFire.description: Until your next turn, when an enemy ship moves into or within Close range of your ship, you may make an immediate attack against them using Daring + Security assisted by the ship's Weapons + Security. You may not take this action at the same time as Evasive Action.

fire.name: Fire
fire.description: Attack an enemy ship or target with the ship's weapons. Roll Control + Security assisted by the ship's Weapons + Security. See Starship Attacks for details.

modulateShields.name: Modulate Shields
modulateShields.description: No roll required. Spend 1 Reserve Power. Until the start of your next turn, the ship's Resistance is increased by 2 against energy weapons.

tractorBeam.name: Tractor Beam
tractorBeam.description: Grab and hold a target in place. Roll Control + Security assisted by the ship's Sensors + Science vs Difficulty 2. On success, the target cannot move until freed. Escaping requires an opposed roll.

aim.name: Aim
aim.description: When you make an Attack this turn, you may re-roll a single d20 on the task roll.

drawItem.name: Draw Item
drawItem.description: Pick up an item within Reach or draw an item you are carrying. If using the item doesn’t require a task to use, you can use it immediately as part of this minor action.

interact.name: Interact
interact.description: Interact with an object in the environment. Complex actions require a major action.

movement.name: Move
movement.description: Move up to one zone, to any point within Medium range. You cannot take this minor action in the same turn as the Sprint major action. If there are any enemies within Reach of you, you cannot perform this action.

prepare.name: Prepare
prepare.description: Some items require this minor action before they can be used, and some major actions require this minor action before they can be attempted. Sometimes items will grant special benefits if this action is used before performing a task.

standDropProne.name: Stand/Drop Prone
standDropProne.description: Drop to the ground, making yourself a smaller target, or stand up from being prone. You cannot Stand and Drop Prone in the same turn.

assist.name: Assist
assist.description: Help 2 other characters per round instead of just 1. You may take this action on their turn if you have not acted already.

attack.name: Attack
attack.description: Attack an enemy or other viable target and attempt to injure them. See Attempting an Attack (page 290) for details.

createTrait.name: Create a Trait
createTrait.description: Roll vs Difficulty 2 (usually) to Create, Change, or Remove a trait. Describe how you're doing it.

direct.name: Direct
direct.description: May only be attempted by one character on each side in a position of authority. Spend 1 Momentum and select one ally who can hear you. They may immediately attempt a single major action with no +1 Difficulty; you Assist them with your Control + Command.

firstAid.name: First Aid
firstAid.description: Attempt to revive a Defeated character within reach. Perform a Daring + Medicine task, Difficulty 2. If successful, the character is no longer Defeated. Alternatively, treat the Injury with a Difficulty equal to its severity. If successful, remove the injury.

guard.name: Guard
guard.description: Defend yourself, preparing for an attack. This is an Insight + Security task, Difficulty 0. Success increases the Difficulty of any attacks against you by 1 until the start of your next turn. +1 Difficulty to guard an ally within Reach instead.

otherTasks.name: Other Tasks
otherTasks.description: Do something not described here. GM will tell you how to do it.

ready.name: Ready
ready.description: Choose another major action and perform that when a specific trigger event of your choice occurs.

sprint.name: Sprint
sprint.description: Move two zones, to any point within Long range. If there is difficult or hazardous terrain, you may attempt a Fitness + Conn task with Difficulty 0 as part of this action, to generate Momentum to cross the terrain.
